caption.text: Your SaaS can look twice as powerful without touching the code
or adding any new features.
And you can even do it starting today.
There’s a psychological bias that will
influence your user’s whole perception of your product,
it’s the contrast effect.
Your brain never perceives anything in absolute terms.
It compares. Based on what it just saw,
based on what you put next to it,
based on what’s in its mind when it looks. In real life,
that’s what happens, for example, when you see a dish at €35
after seeing a dish at €90 on the same menu,
at that moment,
the €35 seems way more reasonable, whereas if you’d only seen the €35,
you might have hesitated.
The information didn’t change, but your perception did.
And in your SaaS or your landing page,
it’s exactly the same.
Users never decide in a vacuum,
they always have a point of comparison,
whether they’re aware of it or not. And that reference point,
you can choose it.
For example, an HR SaaS that asks you in onboarding
how many hours you spend on recruitment
each week.
It’s not to collect data, it’s to create contrast.
When you then see that the tool cuts that down to two hours,
the gain seems huge.
And that’s true even if the promise was already written on the landing page. In that case,
the product hasn’t changed.
What changed is what it’s being perceived against.
Your users never see only your product,
they always see your product in relation to something.
So you might as well choose what.
